title: podman run hangs after /etc/system-fips does not exist on host, not mounting FIPS mode secret
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I tried to migrate my existing docker-compose stack on Fedora 31 to podman using podman-compose, but podman hangs at the start up of one container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the following command as root: `podman --log-level=debug run --name=caddy -d --pod=mirrors-stack-2 -l io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 -l io.podman.compose.project=mirrors-stack-2 -l io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 -l com.docker.compose.container-number=1 -l com.docker.compose.service=caddy -e http_proxy=http://v2ray:8080 -e https_proxy=http://v2ray:8080 -e HTTP_PROXY=http://v2ray:8080 -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://v2ray:8080 -e RSYNC_PROXY=v2ray:8080 --mount type=bind,source=/root/mirrors-stack-2/./caddy/Caddyfile,destination=/etc/Caddyfile,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/mnt/data12T,destination=/mnt,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/root/.acme.sh/mirrors.sjtug.org,destination=/certs,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/root/mirrors-stack-2/./frontend/dist,destination=/mirror-frontend,bind-propagation=z --add-host v2ray:127.0.0.1 --add-host v2ray:127.0.0.1 --add-host git-http-backend:127.0.0.1 --add-host git-http-backend:127.0.0.1 --add-host lug:127.0.0.1 --add-host lug:127.0.0.1 --add-host caddy:127.0.0.1 --add-host caddy:127.0.0.1 caddy:built -conf /etc/Caddyfile -catimeout 30s -email vicluo96@gmail.com -agree --log stdout -port 443`


(v2ray and lug are two other containers running on the machine)

**Describe the results you received:**

It hangs forever:

```

DEBU[0000] Reading configuration file "/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container starts as expected

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body: Given the timing, this is most likely a hang while executing CNI. Can you try adding `--net=host` and see if the container starts to verify this?

Still hangs with:

```
 podman --log-level=debug run --name=caddy -d --pod=mirrors-stack-2 -l io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 -l io.podman.compose.project=mirrors-stack-2 --net=host -l io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 -l com.docker.compose.container-number=1 -l com.docker.compose.service=caddy -e http_proxy=http://v2ray:8080 -e https_proxy=http://v2ray:8080 -e HTTP_PROXY=http://v2ray:8080 -e HTTPS_PROXY=http://v2ray:8080 -e RSYNC_PROXY=v2ray:8080 --mount type=bind,source=/root/mirrors-stack-2/./caddy/Caddyfile,destination=/etc/Caddyfile,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/mnt/data12T,destination=/mnt,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/root/.acme.sh/mirrors.sjtug.org,destination=/certs,bind-propagation=z --mount type=bind,source=/root/mirrors-stack-2/./frontend/dist,destination=/mirror-frontend,bind-propagation=z --add-host v2ray:127.0.0.1 --add-host v2ray:127.0.0.1 --add-host git-http-backend:127.0.0.1 --add-host git-http-backend:127.0.0.1 --add-host lug:127.0.0.1 --add-host lug:127.0.0.1 --add-host caddy:127.0.0.1 --add-host caddy:127.0.0.1 caddy:built -conf /etc/Caddyfile -catimeout 30s -email vicluo96@gmail.com -agree --log stdout -port 2515
```

```
DEBU[0000] unable to find flag net                      
```

That seems properly concerning.
Where are you sourcing the image from? I can't replicate as it's not in any registry I have access to.

Here's the Docker file:
<details>

<summary> Dockerfile </summary>

```
#
# Final stage
#
FROM alpine:3.9
LABEL maintainer "Abiola Ibrahim <abiola89@gmail.com>"

LABEL caddy_version="0.11.5"

RUN apk add --no-cache openssh-client git openssl wget

# install caddy
RUN (wget -qO- https://caddyserver.com/download/linux/amd64\?plugins\=http.cgi,http.cors,http.expires,http.filter,http.git,http.jwt,http.locale,http.login,http.prometheus,http.ratelimit,http.realip,http.reauth,tls.dns.dnspod\&license\=personal\&telemetry\=off | tar xzvC /tmp) && cp /tmp/caddy /usr/bin/caddy

# validate install
RUN /usr/bin/caddy -version
RUN /usr/bin/caddy -plugins

EXPOSE 80 443 2015
VOLUME /root/.caddy /srv
WORKDIR /srv

COPY Caddyfile /etc/Caddyfile
COPY index.html /srv/index.html

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/bin/caddy"]
CMD ["--conf", "/etc/Caddyfile", "--log", "stdout"]
```
</details>
Do other containers work?  Have you tried this rootful as well as rootless?
Other containers worked as expected. This happens in rootful. I'll give rootless a try
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@htfy96 Are you still seeing this in the master branch?
I believe this is fixed, reopen if I am mistaken.
